prompt:
Two-app task: find the invoice email in inbox, then record three
pieces of basic metadata in the spreadsheet.

Step 1 — Email login (required first visit):
Navigate to http://cua_email.web. The landing page is a single
User ID form. Type "user_0001" and click "Create Session" to
enter the inbox.

Step 2 — Find the invoice in inbox:
In the inbox list, locate the email from "Vendor Billing" with
subject "Invoice #2024-001". You do NOT need to open the email
body — everything you need (sender name and subject) is visible
directly in the inbox list row.

Step 3 — Record in spreadsheet:
Navigate to http://cua_spreadsheet.web. The workbook "Book 1"
is already open with an empty "Sheet1". Click the cells and
type the values, pressing Enter to commit each:

- A1: "Sender"           B1: "Vendor Billing"
- A2: "Subject"          B2: "Invoice #2024-001"
- A3: "Folder"           B3: "Inbox"
- A4: "Year"             B4: 2024   (extract the year from the
                                     invoice number "#2024-001")
- A5: "Invoice no"        B5: "2024-001"  (the part after the # in
                                     the invoice number)

Constraints:
- Match the exact text for B1, B2, B3 (no extra spaces, no quotes).
- B4 must be the integer 2024.
- Do not modify any emails (do not flag, archive, delete, open).
- Use ONLY the standard email UI. Do not navigate to /docs, /api/,
  /openapi, /swagger or any developer documentation pages.

  Efficiency tips (you have limited steps — be fast):
  - Find the email fast: use the inbox Search box (magnifier icon at the
    top), type the sender's name, open the matching row. Don't scroll.
  - Fill ALL spreadsheet cells in ONE action: click cell A1 once, then
    type the whole 2-column table as a single block using a Tab between
    the two columns and Enter at the end of each row. Example of a single
    typed value: "Sender<TAB>...<ENTER>Subject<TAB>...<ENTER>..." — this
    populates A1,B1,A2,B2,... in one go. This is far faster and more
    reliable than editing cells one at a time.

source.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Vendor Billing'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject'
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM cells WHERE row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Invoice #2024-001'

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Year' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND (raw_value='2024' OR computed_value='2024') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Invoice no' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='2024-001' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Vendor Billing' AND subject='Invoice #2024-001'

prompt:
Two-app task: find a specific email in the inbox and record its
metadata in the spreadsheet.

Step 1 — Email login:
Navigate to http://cua_email.web and log in as "user_0001"
(single User ID input, then "Create Session").

Step 2 — Find the email:
In the Inbox folder, locate the email from "David Kim" with a
subject about a team lunch ("Team Lunch - This Friday"). The
sender and subject are visible directly in the inbox row — do
NOT open the body.

Step 3 — Record in spreadsheet:
Navigate to http://cua_spreadsheet.web and enter:

- A1: "Sender"   B1: "David Kim"
- A2: "Subject"  B2: the subject text of that email
- A3: "Folder"   B3: "Inbox"
- A4: "Day"  B4: the weekday in the subject (e.g. Friday)
- A5: "Event"  B5: the event type in the subject (one word, e.g. Lunch)

Constraints:
- A1-A5 labels exactly as shown. B1="David Kim", B3="Inbox".
- Do not modify any emails.
- Do NOT navigate to /docs, /api/, /openapi, /swagger.

  Efficiency tips (you have limited steps — be fast):
  - Find the email fast: use the inbox Search box (magnifier icon at the
    top), type the sender's name, open the matching row. Don't scroll.
  - Fill ALL spreadsheet cells in ONE action: click cell A1 once, then
    type the whole 2-column table as a single block using a Tab between
    the two columns and Enter at the end of each row. Example of a single
    typed value: "Sender<TAB>...<ENTER>Subject<TAB>...<ENTER>..." — this
    populates A1,B1,A2,B2,... in one go. This is far faster and more
    reliable than editing cells one at a time.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='David Kim' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value LIKE '%Lunch%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Day' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='friday' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Event' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='lunch' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='David Kim' AND subject='Team Lunch - This Friday'

prompt:
Two-app task: find a specific email in the inbox and record its
metadata in the spreadsheet.

Step 1 — Email login:
Navigate to http://cua_email.web and log in as "user_0001"
(single User ID input, then "Create Session").

Step 2 — Find the email:
In the Inbox folder, locate the email from "Michael Chen" with
the subject "Design Review Meeting - February". The sender and
subject are visible directly in the inbox row — do NOT open the
body.

Step 3 — Record in spreadsheet:
Navigate to http://cua_spreadsheet.web and enter:

- A1: "Sender"   B1: "Michael Chen"
- A2: "Subject"  B2: the subject text of that email
- A3: "Folder"   B3: "Inbox"
- A4: "Month"    B4: the month mentioned in the subject (one
                     word, e.g. the month of the meeting)

Constraints:
- A1-A4 labels exactly as shown.
- B1 = "Michael Chen" exactly. B3 = "Inbox" exactly.
- B4 is the single month word from the subject.
- Do not modify any emails.
- Do NOT navigate to /docs, /api/, /openapi, /swagger.

  Efficiency tips (you have limited steps — be fast):
  - Find the email fast: use the inbox Search box (magnifier icon at the
    top), type the sender's name, open the matching row. Don't scroll.
  - Fill ALL spreadsheet cells in ONE action: click cell A1 once, then
    type the whole 2-column table as a single block using a Tab between
    the two columns and Enter at the end of each row. Example of a single
    typed value: "Sender<TAB>...<ENTER>Subject<TAB>...<ENTER>..." — this
    populates A1,B1,A2,B2,... in one go. This is far faster and more
    reliable than editing cells one at a time.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender'       THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Michael Chen' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value LIKE '%Design Review%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox'  THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Month'    THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='February' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Michael Chen' AND subject='Design Review Meeting - February'

prompt:
Two-app task: find a specific email in the inbox and record its
metadata in the spreadsheet.

Step 1 — Email login:
Navigate to http://cua_email.web and log in as "user_0001"
(single User ID input, then "Create Session").

Step 2 — Find the email:
In the Inbox folder, locate the email from "Jessica Martinez"
with the subject "Marketing Campaign Proposal". The sender and
subject are visible directly in the inbox row — do NOT open the
body.

Step 3 — Record in spreadsheet:
Navigate to http://cua_spreadsheet.web and enter:

- A1: "Sender"   B1: "Jessica Martinez"
- A2: "Subject"  B2: the subject text of that email
- A3: "Folder"   B3: "Inbox"
- A4: "Topic"  B4: the first word of the subject
- A5: "DocType"  B5: the last word of the subject (the document type)

Constraints:
- A1-A5 labels exactly as shown. B1="Jessica Martinez", B3="Inbox".
- Do not modify any emails.
- Do NOT navigate to /docs, /api/, /openapi, /swagger.

  Efficiency tips (you have limited steps — be fast):
  - Find the email fast: use the inbox Search box (magnifier icon at the
    top), type the sender's name, open the matching row. Don't scroll.
  - Fill ALL spreadsheet cells in ONE action: click cell A1 once, then
    type the whole 2-column table as a single block using a Tab between
    the two columns and Enter at the end of each row. Example of a single
    typed value: "Sender<TAB>...<ENTER>Subject<TAB>...<ENTER>..." — this
    populates A1,B1,A2,B2,... in one go. This is far faster and more
    reliable than editing cells one at a time.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Jessica Martinez' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value LIKE '%Marketing%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Topic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='marketing' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='DocType' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='proposal' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Jessica Martinez' AND subject='Marketing Campaign Proposal'

prompt:
Two-app task: find a specific email in the inbox and record its
metadata in the spreadsheet.

Step 1 — Email login:
Navigate to http://cua_email.web and log in as "user_0001"
(single User ID input, then "Create Session").

Step 2 — Find the email:
In the Inbox folder, locate the email from "Carlos Mendez" with
a subject about incident retrospective notes ("Re: Incident
retrospective notes"). The sender and subject are visible
directly in the inbox row — do NOT open the body.

Step 3 — Record in spreadsheet:
Navigate to http://cua_spreadsheet.web and enter:

- A1: "Sender"   B1: "Carlos Mendez"
- A2: "Subject"  B2: the exact subject text, including the 'Re:' prefix
- A3: "Folder"   B3: "Inbox"
- A4: "Topic"  B4: the first word after 'Re:' in the subject (e.g. Incident)
- A5: "DocType"  B5: the last word of the subject

Constraints:
- A1-A5 labels exactly as shown. B1="Carlos Mendez", B3="Inbox".
- Do not modify any emails.
- Do NOT navigate to /docs, /api/, /openapi, /swagger.

  Efficiency tips (you have limited steps — be fast):
  - Find the email fast: use the inbox Search box (magnifier icon at the
    top), type the sender's name, open the matching row. Don't scroll.
  - Fill ALL spreadsheet cells in ONE action: click cell A1 once, then
    type the whole 2-column table as a single block using a Tab between
    the two columns and Enter at the end of each row. Example of a single
    typed value: "Sender<TAB>...<ENTER>Subject<TAB>...<ENTER>..." — this
    populates A1,B1,A2,B2,... in one go. This is far faster and more
    reliable than editing cells one at a time.

source.query:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Sender' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a1,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=0 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Carlos Mendez' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b1
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Subject' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a2,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=1 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Re: Incident retrospective notes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b2
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Folder' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a3,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=2 AND col_index=1 AND raw_value='Inbox' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b3
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='Topic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a4,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=3 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='incident' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b4
FROM cells

SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=0 AND raw_value='DocType' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS a5,
  SUM(CASE WHEN row_index=4 AND col_index=1 AND LOWER(TRIM(raw_value))='notes' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS b5
FROM cells
SELECT folderId FROM emails WHERE userId='user_0001' AND fromName='Carlos Mendez' AND subject='Re: Incident retrospective notes'
